page: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX-512 VNNI for vector neural network instructions), AES cryptography, and FMA. The server is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. It includes 2458 GB of local NVMe storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 59595.68 and a Passmark CPU extended instructions test score of 56731.97, both placing in the top 25% tier. Memory performance is also robust, showing strong uncached large-block read and write bandwidths of 207.3 GB/sec and 95.23 GB/sec respectively, alongside a strong Passmark memory score of 2809.39. However, single-core performance remains moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1301.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1468.09 falling into the average category. Database operations perform strongly, registering 22567.01 on Passmark database benchmarks.

The server exhibits a clear performance tradeoff when handling large-scale machine learning tasks. While prompt processing for a medium 7B LLM model is average at 99.69 tokens/sec, text generation for a large 70B model is poor, yielding only 2.33 tokens/sec due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. Qualitatively, the D64ds_v4 offers solid cost efficiency for workloads that require high resource density and local storage without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. It is highly suitable for relational databases, multi-threaded enterprise applications, data compression pipelines, and general-purpose web serving.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores) running at 2.6 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, 2458 GB of local storage, and supports Premium Storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes AVX-512 and AES instruction sets. Benchmarks reveal strong multi-core CPU performance and high uncached memory bandwidth, alongside strong database operation capabilities. However, single-core performance and large LLM text generation speeds are average to poor, highlighting a tradeoff for single-threaded or heavy AI workloads. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for resource-dense workloads, making it well-suited for database hosting, multi-threaded enterprise applications, and general-purpose web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v4 is a general-purpose server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v4 is a 64-vCPU general-purpose server with 256 GB RAM and 2458 GB local storage, optimized for multi-core enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced 64-vCPU virtual machine with high-capacity local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance for database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8272CL
Includes 2458 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core CPU and uncached memory bandwidth performance
Excellent database operation throughput and multi-threaded processing
Limited single-core performance and poor large-model LLM text generation
Best suited for relational databases, enterprise applications, and web serving
